key_insight: A pretrained foundation-model MLIP can be adaptively fine-tuned during evolutionary crystal structure prediction to cut DFT labeling costs while still finding stable new phases.
engineering_relevance: This provides a more scalable workflow for computational materials discovery in complex multicomponent systems, especially when high-pressure phase space is expensive to search.
method: Start from a pretrained machine-learning interatomic potential.
Run evolutionary crystal structure search with the MLIP to explore candidates quickly.
Iteratively select compact, representative, physically relevant structures for DFT labeling and fine-tune the model.
evidence_signal: The workflow reproduced the known low-pressure convex hull and predicted a new compound, Ca6FeNi, that becomes thermodynamically stable above 100 GPa.
watch_item: Performance still depends on the quality of the pretrained model and on whether the adaptive selection captures diverse, physically important structures rather than missing rare but stable candidates.
paper_title: Adaptive fine-tuning of foundation models for crystal structure prediction: Discovery of high-pressure phases in the CaFeNi system
